behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-to-text model output quality (speaker diarization/labeling), which mocks can hide. This is a single model-quality boundary, not a multi-agent chain, so Agentic over Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API and assert transcript speaker labels are present and consistently mapped per utterance for downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parsing/mapping logic on transcript text into a local structure; no required OS/network/subprocess boundary and no required real model judgment in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic transcript snippets with explicit assignments into the parsing function/object and assert extracted `{assignee, task_text}` records match expected schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on LLM output content (coverage/completeness of action items by assignee). Stubbed responses would trivially pass and miss real omission failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior can be validated with one real summarization decision from prepared transcript input.
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one returned action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly scopes to routing already-identified decisions into schema fields, which is local transformation logic. No real infra or model-output quality is required for this boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the decision-routing logic with fixtures containing pre-identified decision objects and assert output populates `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real LLM chooses to extract/place decision content correctly, which is model judgment and can vary across runs. Rejected Workflow because a single summarization pass can exercise this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs containing explicit decisions through the live summarizer and assert those decisions are present in returned `decisions` entries, not only in free-text narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step sequencing (split -> per-segment processing -> merge) and includes repeated agentic calls whose combined outputs must be coherent. Single-step Agentic would miss handoff/merge failures across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute recording end-to-end with production segmentation enabled; verify sequential segment execution, successful merge, and final summary coherence/structure across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is full pipeline completion over multiple stages including at least transcription and summarization model calls. Real cross-stage orchestration and multiple agentic steps are intrinsic to the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run using a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery and assert unattended completion plus valid structured output payload.
